psychology_remaining_courses.title: Industrial/Organizational Psychology
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Application of psychological principles to workplace settings including employee selection, performance management, leadership, and organizational behavior. Students learn to improve workplace effectiveness and employee well-being.
learning_objectives: Apply psychological principles to workplace issues
Understand employee selection and assessment methods
Analyze job performance and performance management
Evaluate leadership and team effectiveness
Address workplace stress and employee well-being

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Health Psychology
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Examination of psychological factors in health and illness including stress, coping, health behaviors, and the mind-body connection. Students learn about the role of psychology in promoting health and preventing disease.
learning_objectives: Understand psychological factors in health and illness
Analyze stress and coping mechanisms
Evaluate health behavior change theories and interventions
Examine the role of psychology in medical settings
Apply health psychology principles to prevention and treatment
covered_materials: Biopsychosocial model of health and illness
Cultural factors in health and healthcare

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Cross-Cultural Psychology
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Study of cultural influences on psychological processes including cognition, emotion, personality, and social behavior. Students examine how culture shapes human experience and behavior across different societies.
learning_objectives: Understand cultural influences on psychological processes
Compare psychological phenomena across cultures
Analyze cultural factors in personality and social behavior
Evaluate cross-cultural research methods and challenges
Apply cultural competence to psychological practice
covered_materials: Cultural variations in cognition and perception
Culture and emotion, motivation, and personality
Cultural factors in mental health and psychopathology

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Psychological Testing and Assessment
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Principles and practices of psychological testing including test construction, reliability, validity, and interpretation. Students learn to select, administer, and interpret psychological tests for various purposes.
learning_objectives: Understand principles of psychological test construction
Evaluate test reliability and validity
Select appropriate tests for specific purposes
Administer and score psychological tests
Interpret test results and write assessment reports
covered_materials: Ethical and legal issues in testing

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Ethics in Psychology
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Comprehensive study of ethical principles and professional standards in psychology including research ethics, therapeutic relationships, and professional conduct. Students learn to make ethical decisions in psychological practice.
learning_objectives: Understand ethical principles and professional standards
Apply ethical decision-making frameworks
Analyze ethical dilemmas in research and practice
Understand legal and regulatory requirements
Develop professional identity and ethical awareness
covered_materials: APA Ethics Code and professional standards

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Advanced Research Seminar
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Advanced research methods and current topics in psychology including literature review, research proposal development, and presentation of research findings. Students conduct independent research projects under faculty supervision.
learning_objectives: Conduct comprehensive literature reviews
Develop research proposals and hypotheses
Apply advanced research methodologies
Analyze and interpret complex research data
Present research findings professionally
covered_materials: Current issues and trends in psychology

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Introduction to Neuroscience
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Introduction to the structure and function of the nervous system including neurons, brain anatomy, and neural circuits. Students learn fundamental neuroscience concepts relevant to understanding behavior and cognition.
learning_objectives: Understand basic neuron structure and function
Learn major brain structures and their functions
Explain neural communication and neurotransmission
Understand neural development and plasticity
Apply neuroscience concepts to psychological phenomena

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Introduction to Sociology
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Introduction to sociological perspectives on human behavior and social institutions including culture, socialization, social groups, and social inequality. Students learn to analyze social phenomena from sociological viewpoints.
learning_objectives: Understand major sociological theories and perspectives
Analyze social institutions and their functions
Examine social stratification and inequality
Understand socialization and social interaction processes
Apply sociological imagination to contemporary issues

psychology_remaining_courses.title: Philosophy of Mind
psychology_remaining_courses.description: Philosophical examination of the nature of mind, consciousness, and mental states including the mind-body problem, free will, and personal identity. Students explore fundamental questions about the nature of mental phenomena.
learning_objectives: Understand major philosophical problems about mind and consciousness
Analyze the mind-body problem and proposed solutions
Examine theories of consciousness and mental states
Evaluate arguments about free will and determinism
Apply philosophical reasoning to questions about mind
covered_materials: The mind-body problem and dualism vs. materialism
Functionalism and computational theories of mind
